name: Sierra de Ricote and La Navela Loop
name_original: Sierras de Ricote y La Navela – Ruta Circular
description: This difficult 21.3-mile mountain biking route in Sierras de Ricote y La Navela offers diverse terrain and stunning views, requiring advance
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Sierra de Ricote traverse a diverse landscape characterized by arid mountain ranges, lush riverine areas, and extensive forest trails. The region features significant elevation changes, from the fertile plains of the Ricote Valley along the Segura River to higher points offering panoramic views. This varied terrain provides a dynamic environment for mountain biking, with routes winding through orchards, along rivers, and across rugged mountain slopes. Best mountain bike trails around Sierra de Ricote The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Ricote and La Navela Loop, a 21.3 miles (34.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain across the Sierra de Ricote. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of the Sierra de la Pila – View of Piedra Lisa loop from Ricote, a difficult 22.0 miles (35.4 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the surrounding sierras and takes approximately 5 hours 3 minutes. Local mountain bikers also love the El Gurugú – Ricote Valley loop from Blanca, a 26.2 miles (42.2 km) trail leading through the Ricote Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. This moderate route combines riverside scenery with valley views. Mountain biking around Sierra de Ricote is defined by arid mountain ranges, lush riverine areas, and extensive forest trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Sierra de Ricote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 1,400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sierra de Ricote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sierra de Ricote?
A: There are nearly 80 mountain bike trails recorded in Sierra de Ricote on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes 9 easy routes, 49 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes, offering a diverse range of experiences from riverside paths to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The terrain in Sierra de Ricote is highly diverse, featuring arid mountain ranges, lush riverine areas, and extensive forest trails. You'll find routes winding through fertile orchards along the Segura River, as well as trails with significant elevation gains across rugged mountain slopes, providing a dynamic and varied riding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, Sierra de Ricote offers several family-friendly options, particularly along the River Segura through orchards and villages. These routes tend to be easier and flatter. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarral River Beach – Puente de Alambre loop from Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.6 km long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Absolutely. Many of the mountain bike trails in Sierra de Ricote are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e921124420/sierra-de-ricote-and-la-navela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ricote and La Navela Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gurugú – Ricote Valley loop from Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic riverside and valley views.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while mountain biking in the region?
A: Sierra de Ricote is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2651129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a small oasis near Roman sites, or experience the fun zigzag descent of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/994588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The 13 Curves&lt;/a&gt;. High points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4827909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeces&lt;/a&gt; offer spectacular panoramic views of the entire region.

Q: Are there good viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, the region is known for its stunning viewpoints. You can find expansive vistas from places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de la Pila&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5012552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Piedra Lisa&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ricote Valley from Collado de la Calera&lt;/a&gt;. These spots provide excellent opportunities to take in the diverse landscapes of the valley and surrounding mountains.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Mountain biking in the Ricote Valley is possible year-round. Spring is particularly beautiful with the peach blossoms, offering vibrant scenery. The cooler months, from autumn through spring, are generally ideal for tackling the lower routes and enjoying comfortable temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if riding during this season.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sierra de Ricote are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush river valleys to arid mountains, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Definitely. Sierra de Ricote offers 19 difficult routes designed for experienced mountain bikers. These trails feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40363924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de la Pila – View of Piedra Lisa loop from Ricote&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35.4 km with over 1000 meters of ascent, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27637335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solvente Viewpoint – Jarral River Beach loop from Villanueva del Río Segura&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I explore historical sites or cultural heritage while mountain biking in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Ricote region is rich in Moorish heritage and Roman sites. Many trails pass through areas with ancient irrigation systems, medieval castles, and charming villages, allowing you to combine your ride with cultural exploration. The contrast between the natural landscapes and historical elements adds a unique dimension to your mountain biking experience.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the River Segura?
A: Yes, the Segura River is a central feature of the Ricote Valley, and many trails follow its course. These routes often lead through fertile orchards and picturesque villages, providing a more gentle riding experience compared to the mountain trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gurugú – Ricote Valley loop from Blanca&lt;/a&gt; is a good example, combining riverside scenery with valley views.
tips: Incredible views of one of the most vertical walls of the Ricote Valley

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Sierras de Ricote y La Navela – Ruta Circular is a challenging ride that typically takes around 3 hours and 48 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 34.2 km loop.

faqs.question: Is the Sierras de Ricote y La Navela – Ruta Circular suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for significant elevation gains and technical sections, including narrow singletrack and potentially needing to push their bikes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain, transitioning from lush riverine environments along the River Segura to arid mountain landscapes. You'll encounter a mix of well-maintained forest tracks, trails with loose stones, extensive forest trails, arid ravines, and cliffs. Expect narrow, almost relentless singletrack and wider tracks that can have deteriorated sections, plus pronounced ramps leading up to Ricote.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy magnificent views of the Sierra and Valle de Ricote, characterized by verdant river valleys and arid mountain ranges. Notable highlights include the Old Trail of the Sierra de Ricote and La Navela, the View of Piedra Lisa, and the View from the Ricote Watchtower. The route also passes through areas with pine trees, kermes oak, rosemary, and thyme.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierras de Ricote y La Navela – Ruta Circular?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route often starts near villages like Ulea or Ricote. It's advisable to check the Komoot tour page or local maps for designated parking spots in these areas before your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the arid mountain landscapes and potential for high temperatures, the best time to ride this route is typically during the cooler months, such as spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). This avoids the intense summer heat and offers more pleasant riding conditions.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of Barranco del Pozo, Sendero del Collado de la Madera, senda de la bermeja, Senda de la Fuente de la Facarola, Senda Cañada de Priego, Senda de la Madera, Senda de la Calera, Senda de la Cabaña, Senda Serranos, and Senda Collado de las Pilas.

faqs.question: Is the Sierras de Ricote y La Navela – Ruta Circular dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the region, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially as this area is a Zona de Especial Protección para Aves (Special Protection Area for Birds). The challenging terrain and potential for long stretches without water also mean you should consider your dog's fitness and carry extra water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierras de Ricote y La Navela is designated as a Zona de Especial Protección para Aves (Special Protection Area for Birds). While there are typically no entrance fees for accessing the natural park itself, it's always good practice to check local regulations for any specific permits required for mountain biking or activities within protected areas. Generally, responsible use and adherence to trail rules are expected.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the route?
faqs.answer: A practical note for riders is the potential absence of water along significant sections of the route. It is highly recommended to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your ride, especially given the challenging nature and potential for arid conditions.

faqs.question: What should I pack for mountain biking this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, pack plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate safety gear. Sun protection is essential due to exposed sections. A map or GPS device is also recommended for navigation.
